chunk_summary: Digital tools and structured training improve growth monitoring accuracy using WHO/IAP charts, with innovations like mobile apps, EMR modules, and community-facing technologies enhancing quality metrics and reducing errors.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of digital tools like WHO Anthro software and IAP-based apps in growth monitoring.
Identify key features of effective EMR/EHR growth modules for pediatric care.
Explore community-level innovations like tablet-based monitoring and SMS/IVR systems for follow-up.
Recognize the importance of structured training and quality metrics in anthropometric accuracy.
key_facts: WHO Anthro software calculates percentiles and z-scores for ages 0–5 years, while AnthroPlus covers ages 5–19 years.
EMR/EHR growth modules should include unit checks, biologically implausible value flags, and centile-channel crossing alerts.
India uses mobile apps like AnthroCal for rapid BMI and percentile interpretation in school-age children.
Community innovations include tablet-based growth monitoring under POSHAN pilots and SMS/IVR systems for follow-up reminders.
Structured training and periodic refreshers improve anthropometric proficiency more than software upgrades.
A practical quality metric is the rate of 'outlier rechecks' completed during the same visit.
reference_quote: Structured training with periodic refreshers improves anthropometric proficiency and reduces misclassification; simple clinic checklists often deliver a larger accuracy gain than any software upgrade.

sub_subtopic: DIGITAL TOOLS AND QUALITY IMPROVEMENT METHODS
